skill.name: master-orchestrator
skill.description: Orchestrates the Defou Workflow Agent pipeline from TopHub trend discovery to draft generation and viral verification.
skill.summary: Runs a two-stage content pipeline for trend-based article production and review.
skill.author: wangdefou-dev

security_audit.summary: Static analysis over-reported Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution, including skill names and output paths. The skill does document shell commands and global .env reuse, which create moderate risk in a community package because the invoked implementation was not present in the audited file.

medium_findings.title: Project Command Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The static backtick findings at the command section are not Ruby execution, but the skill instructs users to run npm and npx commands. In a community skill, these commands can execute arbitrary project code, and the implementation script was not present in the audited file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command text is explicit and directly actionable. Confidence is below very high because no executable implementation file was included in the audited file set.

medium_findings.title: Global Environment Configuration Reuse
medium_findings.description: The skill says it reuses the project's global .env configuration. This may expose secrets to the orchestrated scripts, although no evidence of network exfiltration or secret misuse was found in the audited file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The .env dependency is stated plainly in the documentation. The risk depends on unseen project scripts, so this remains a moderate-confidence concern.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick False Positives
low_findings.description: Several static findings are Markdown code spans for skill names or output directories, not executable Ruby or shell backtick expressions. These include referenced skills and output paths documented for users.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding lines show documentation formatting, not executable code. The pattern is a clear scanner false positive for these locations.

low_findings.title: High Entropy and Weak Crypto Findings Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The high entropy and weak cryptographic algorithm findings were not supported by the visible text in the audited file. No binary content, encrypted payload, or cryptographic implementation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain front matter and a Chinese description. This supports a false positive, although only the single listed file was available for review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Shell Command Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells users to invoke project scripts through npm and ts-node. This is a legitimate workflow pattern, but users should inspect the project scripts before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The executable commands are present in the documentation. The actual runtime behavior cannot be confirmed from SKILL.md alone.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment File Dependency
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on the project's global .env configuration. This can increase secret exposure if downstream scripts read or transmit environment variables.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dependency is documented directly. No confirmed misuse was found in the audited file.

content.value_statement: Trend-based content workflows often require separate discovery, drafting, and review steps. This skill coordinates the Defou Workflow Agent pipeline so drafts move from TopHub topics to viral verification.
actual_capabilities: Coordinates a content generation engine and a quality verification engine in sequence.
Documents TopHub trend collection as the first stage of the workflow.
Selects ten topics described as having strong viral potential.
Generates drafts in a Defou x Stanley style through the referenced generation skill.
Runs a six-dimension viral scoring review through the referenced verification skill.
Directs users to draft and final output folders for generated articles.
limitations: The audited package contains only SKILL.md, so the runtime script behavior was not verified.
It depends on other project skills named in the documentation.
It reuses the project's global .env configuration.
The workflow is tailored to Chinese trend content and a Defou x Stanley writing style.

use_cases.title: Daily Trend Drafting
use_cases.description: Run the documented pipeline to turn current TopHub topics into article drafts and reviewed final posts.

use_cases.title: Editorial Quality Review
use_cases.description: Use the verification stage to score draft articles across viral factors before publication.

use_cases.title: Automated Content Workflow Testing
use_cases.description: Validate how a multi-skill content pipeline passes outputs from generation to final review.

prompt_templates.title: Run the Basic Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use master-orchestrator to run the documented content workflow and show me where the final reviewed posts are saved.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Generated Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the workflow, then summarize the selected topics, draft locations, and final reviewed output locations.

prompt_templates.title: Check Readiness Before Running
prompt_templates.prompt: Before using master-orchestrator, inspect the required project commands, expected output folders, and environment configuration dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Workflow Output Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the orchestrated workflow, compare the drafts with the verified final posts, and identify the strongest improvements made by the review stage.

output_examples.input: Run the master orchestrator for today's content workflow.
output_examples.output: The workflow selects trending topics, generates initial Defou x Stanley drafts, and places verified final posts in the documented output folder.

output_examples.input: Review the final outputs from the orchestrated pipeline.
output: Selected topics are grouped by viral potential.
Drafts are checked with six viral quality dimensions.
Final posts include optimization guidance for publication.

output_examples.input: Prepare a preflight report before execution.
output_examples.output: The report lists the required npm or ts-node command, expected output folders, referenced skills, and the global .env dependency.

best_practices: Inspect project scripts before running the documented npm or ts-node command.
Use a limited .env file with only the secrets required for this workflow.
Review final posts manually before publishing them to public channels.
anti_patterns: Do not run the commands in an untrusted project without reading the scripts first.
Do not store broad production credentials in the global .env file used by this workflow.
Do not publish generated posts without checking accuracy, tone, and source relevance.

faq.question: What does this skill orchestrate?
faq.answer: It connects trend collection, content draft generation, and viral quality verification in the Defou Workflow Agent project.

faq.question: Does the audited skill include executable source code?
faq.answer: No. The audited file set contains SKILL.md, which documents commands and expected workflow behavior.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Where are final posts expected to appear?
faq.answer: The documentation points users to defou-workflow-agent/outputs/viral-verified-posts/ for final reviewed articles.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill documents local command execution and .env reuse, but no malicious code was found in the audited SKILL.md file.

faq.question: What should I check before using it?
faq.answer: Inspect the npm scripts, the ts-node target, referenced skills, output folders, and the secrets available through .env.
